title: rubus rudis microgene scopulorum
intro: Rubus rudis microgene scopulorum, also known as the Scouler's blackberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus rudis plant, a type of bramble or blackberry native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant contains anthocyanins, which contribute to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus rudis microgene scopulorum may include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these benefits. Rubus rudis microgene scopulorum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, although commercial herbal preparations of this specific subspecies may be difficult to find due to its limited availability.
